page: Amazon Web Services m6id.24xlarge is a general-purpose instance type powered by the Nitro hypervisor. It features an x86_64 architecture with 96 vCPUs and 48 physical cores derived from an Intel Xeon 8375C processor running at 3.5 GHz. The system is equipped with 384.0 GB of DDR4 memory operating at 3 GHz, providing a balanced allocation of 4.0 GB of RAM per physical core. Local storage is bundled, consisting of 5700 GB of NVMe SSD instance store volumes. Network connectivity delivers a baseline bandwidth of 38 Gbps.

Benchmark data reveals strong multi-core performance, with a stress-ng multi-core score of 92888.69 and a Geekbench multi-core score of 17559.0. Memory bandwidth is also in the top tier, reaching 4972.07 GB/sec for small block read-only cached operations and 329.31 GB/sec for large block uncached reads. Single-core performance is moderate, as indicated by average ratings in stress-ng single-core and Geekbench single-core tests. The instance demonstrates strong capabilities in software compilation and ray tracing.

For database and web serving workloads, the instance achieves 90.26 million operations per second in Redis SET benchmarks and a static web serving throughput of 68.96 GB/sec. Large language model inference performance varies; while prompt processing for a small 135M model is strong at 2331.48 tokens per second, text generation for a large 70B model is weak, yielding only 2.58 tokens per second. This highlights a performance tradeoff when executing large-scale generative AI tasks without dedicated GPU acceleration.

From a cost-efficiency perspective, the integration of high-capacity local NVMe SSD storage alongside a high-density vCPU configuration provides a balanced resource profile for data-intensive applications. This setup reduces the need for external block storage attachments for temporary data. The hardware profile is suited for relational databases, Redis caching layers, software build systems, and web serving applications.
description: Amazon Web Services m6id.24xlarge is an x86_64 general-purpose instance featuring 96 vCPUs and 48 physical cores powered by an Intel Xeon 8375C processor running at 3.5 GHz. It is equipped with 384.0 GB of DDR4 RAM and 5700 GB of local NVMe SSD storage, operating on the Nitro hypervisor with a 38 Gbps baseline network bandwidth. Benchmarks indicate strong multi-core processing and memory bandwidth, alongside high throughput for database operations, such as 90.26 million Redis SET operations per second. However, single-core performance remains average, and large language model text generation is weak due to the lack of GPU acceleration. This instance offers qualitative cost efficiency for resource-dense workloads by bundling substantial local NVMe storage with high vCPU counts. It is suited for databases, caching layers, software compilation, and web serving.
og_description: Amazon Web Services m6id.24xlarge is an x86_64 instance with 96 vCPUs, 384 GB RAM, and 5700 GB local NVMe SSD storage, delivering strong multi-core performance and high database throughput.
meta_description: Amazon Web Services m6id.24xlarge features 96 vCPUs, 384 GB RAM, and 5700 GB NVMe SSD storage, optimized for databases and multi-core workloads.
tagline: A balanced x86_64 platform bundling high vCPU density with 5.7 TB of local NVMe storage for data-intensive workloads.
bullet_points: Intel Xeon 8375C processor with 96 vCPUs and 384 GB DDR4 RAM
Bundled 5700 GB of high-speed local NVMe SSD storage
Strong multi-core execution and memory bandwidth benchmarks
38 Gbps baseline network bandwidth via Nitro hypervisor
Suited for databases, Redis caching, and software compilation
